etymology.description: 力(力) (lì, "strength") + 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") -> exertion with additional effort or breath -> add
etymology.interpretation: The character 加 (jiā, "add") combines 力(力) (lì, "strength"), indicating physical effort or force, with 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), suggesting either verbal command or the exertion of breath when effort is applied. This combination evokes the image of putting in more effort, hence the meaning "add" as in adding effort or increasing in quantity. The evolution from the effortful action to 'add' bridges the concept of augmenting or increasing.

examples.chinese: 请在茶里加些糖(請在茶裡加些糖)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng zài chá lǐ jiā xiē táng.
examples.english: Please add some sugar to the tea.

examples.chinese: 你可以加我为好友(你可以加我為好友)吗？
examples.pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ jiā wǒ wéi hǎoyǒu ma?
examples.english: Can you add me as a friend?

examples.chinese: 我们需要加倍努力(我們需要加倍努力)，才能完成这个项目(才能完成這個項目)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào jiābèi nǔlì, cái néng wánchéng zhège xiàngmù.
examples.english: We need to double our efforts in order to complete this project.
